item.title: Re: Licensing Junior Staff Post - Info
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: PublicDomainPicture.net
License: Public Domain
Title: Busy Street
Author: Petr Kratochvil
Release year: N/A

item.title: Licensing Junior Staff Post - Info
item.content:encoded: The image in this article has been sourced to this stock video https://www.shutterstock.com/de/video/clip-12159989?irgwc=1&utm_medium=Affiliate&utm_campaign=Vectors123.com&utm_source=44866&utm_term= which is not compliant with the sites license. IT has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The one thing bothering me was not being able to find -1, and I can't believe I missed the point there. This is a really neat original monster kind of skip, and we don't get a lot of those right now. So Imma go back and upvote. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …and turned my no-vote into an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After thinking about it, I agree with you. Have removed the journal for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That journal really weighs the piece down. It's a rote character sketch of an individual combining two tropes the wiki already has far too many examples of: the angry, petulant artist with nothing meaningful to say who replaces talent with anomalies; and the idiot who somehow acquires magical powers which he immediately proves too stupid to use and accidentally kills himself. Adrian even manages to be a completely overblown version of these characters - the French stuff and the 'there's no rule against magic' line are ridiculously over the top. It doesn't add anything to the article, destroys the mystery, and makes us sit through a cliched stock character rambling in a really annoying fashion.

The final note's particularly bad. It's like finding a dead man trapped under the wheel of his car in the garage and then finding a note on the table saying 'Hey honey I've gone out to fixed the car - I know you said I should replace our old wheel jack, but I'm sure it'll be fine!' It explains, in a completely inorganic fashion, exactly the specifics of the death and nothing else, and so comes off as contrived.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can cut the journal if people agree it's not necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a portion of the article concerning that particular situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the articles content, but cannot seem to identify the -1 instance in the photo. Perhaps I'm just blind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This didn't need an explanation nearly as clear as the one it got — it would be delightfully fucked and weird, but instead we got this douchebag. Feels like a Series 2 throwback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The author uploaded 2 files that seem to be the exact same, so maybe 2452-1 is in the one that is not used?

Just making hotshots around here

Never mind, the other file got deleted.
Still a good skip though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only concern is that eventually, everyone in these photos will be dead, anyway. What, then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When this occurs, SCP-2452-1 will disappear from every instance of SCP-2452, as well as all copies, and physically manifest in the vicinity of an individual portrayed in the destroyed photograph.

▼ open me
▲ close me

(the article and thus the caption were written when -1 was present and contained — one of the original photos has just been destroyed, intentionally or not)

I dug this one quite a bit. Ending it with another series of explain-y journal entries ehhhh, but I do like how you pushed hard just how much of an art-dick Adrian…was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't find it either. We can be blind together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This skip is really good, my only problem with it though is I can't seem to find 2452-1 in the photo. Probably just being blind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An incident report about 2452-1's strangulation spree would be a nice touch.
